2605.30104.title: SEAL: Can Saturated Benchmarks Be Revived by LLM-as-a-Meta-Judge?
2605.30104.paper_summary: Widely used language-model benchmarks are increasingly saturated, with frontier systems often receiving near-tied scores that standard metrics cannot resolve. Rather than constructing harder alternatives, we ask whether existing tasks can be made informative again through improved evaluation over the same candidate outputs. Therefore, we present Seeded Elimination with Adaptive LLM-as-a-Meta-Judge, a self-improving evaluation protocol for extracting latent ranking signal from saturated benchmarks. SEAL seeds candidate outputs into a single elimination and evaluates each match with task-level principles plus self-improving checklist criteria. We evaluate SEAL on multiple saturated benchmarks covering code generation, mathematical reasoning, knowledge-intensive question answering, and tool-use agent task completion. Across these settings, SEAL improves the ranking-accuracy--latency trade-off over competing protocols, attaining 0.83--1.00 Spearman agreement with full pairwise judging and 4/4 top-1 agreement, while requiring only 11.89 calls per task compared with 28.00 for full pairwise evaluation.
2605.30104.paper_summary_zh: 廣泛使用的語言模型基準正逐漸飽和，前沿系統經常獲得接近平局的分數，而標準指標無法解決這一問題。與其構建更難的替代方案，我們不如問問是否可以通過對相同候選輸出進行改進評估，使現有任務再次變得有意義。因此，我們提出了帶有自適應 LLM 作為元評判的種子淘汰法（Seeded Elimination with Adaptive LLM-as-a-Meta-Judge），這是一種自我改進的評估協議，用於從飽和基準中提取潛在的排名信號。SEAL 將候選輸出種子化到單一淘汰賽中，並根據任務級原則加上自我改進的檢查清單標準來評估每場比賽。我們在多個涵蓋代碼生成、數學推理、知識密集型問答和工具使用代理任務完成的飽和基準上評估 SEAL。在這些設置中，SEAL 改進了與競爭協議相比的排名準確性與延遲的權衡，達到 0.83 到 1.00 的斯皮爾曼一致性，並在全配對評判中獲得 4/4 的前 1 一致性，同時每個任務僅需 11.89 次調用，而全配對評估則需 28.00 次。
2605.30104.author: Jiamin Chen et.al.
2605.30104.authors: Jiamin Chen, Yidi Wu, Qiexiang Wang, Qianben Chen, Yuchen Li, Yansen Zhang, Xiaokun Zhang, Wangchunshu Zhou, Chen Ma
